- Deletion errors (omission of characters, especially in ligature-rich scripts)
- Segmentation errors (incorrect splitting or merging of words, lines, or columns, often due to irregular historical layouts)
- Script misclassification (e.g., confusion between Bengali and English scripts in mixed-script documents)

- Tokenization errors (erroneous splitting or joining of tokens, especially at line breaks or hyphenated words)
- Contextual misrecognition (OCR errors exacerbated by historical spelling variants or archaic typefaces)
- Ambiguous glyphs (characters that are visually similar in historical fonts, leading to systematic misrecognition, such as “ſ” (long s) for “f” or “i” for “l”)
- Noise from marginalia, stamps, or bleed-through (non-textual artifacts misrecognized as text)

These cases are documented in the “summary statistics and illustrative cases” section, with examples and confusion matrices provided for each error type.
